text:
Once upon a time in a small medieval village, there lived a young woman named Melisende. She was known for her beauty, her talent for embroidery, and her modest ways. Melisende had an unusual dream every night: she would dream of dancing amidst ancient ruins, surrounded by voices singing a forgotten melody.

One day, as Melisende was stitching a delicate design under the glow of a lantern, she heard a soft tune that was unlike anything she'd heard before. Intrigued, she leaned closer to the tapestry frame. What she heard was enchanting, a melody so beautiful it seemed woven into the fabric itself.

Determined to uncover the source of this song, she gathered berries and leaves for an elixir capable of catching tunes. Late into the night, she concocted a potion and danced around the ruins near her cottage, humming and singing the melody. To her astonishment, the night air carried echoes of her song far beyond the village.
